In fairness, the scale is fairly consistent, but it's also going to be determined by your clothes, how much water you're holding, your meals and whatnot. But 2 pounds is fair to say, they gained a little bit. Now building muscle is not nearly as easy as losing fat, believe me. But the good news is the more muscle you have, the easier it is to burn fat. So building muscle is really what we want to do. Because muscle is dense and it's a lot smaller than fat. So if this is 1 pound of fat, 1 pound of muscle may be this big and weigh the same because it's a much denser substance. It also gives you energy, gives you strength. And you're doing weightlifting, you're doing a lot of squatting, you're doing things that stimulate muscle. We're not getting on a treadmill here and just out to burn calories. Foundations is geared to get you there, but like now that you're unlimited, it's like, we're not going to tell you come three days a week. Come as much as you like. With that said, listen to your body. You do need rest days. If you're exceptionally sore, you feel broken down, your performance starts to take a dive.
